description: RAG retrieval ablation QA set. Each query targets content that high-quality structured parsing (MinerU) preserves but naive text extraction (PyMuPDF) garbles: display equations with sub/superscripts, multi-cell tables, and multi-column body text. A retrieved chunk counts as relevant if its normalized text (lowercased, alphanumerics only) contains ANY string in answer_norm. The same relevance test is applied to both corpora, so a hit is earned, not assumed.

queries.query: What is the Burgers equation with viscosity used in the data-driven solution example, including the 0.01/pi coefficient?
How is the mean squared error loss decomposed into a supervised data term and a physics residual term?
What is the general parametrized nonlinear partial differential equation form with the nonlinear operator N acting on u?
What relative L2-norm prediction error did the physics-informed network achieve on the Burgers equation?
How many parameters, layers, and neurons per layer were used for the Burgers data-driven solution network?
Which quasi-Newton full-batch optimization algorithm was used to minimize the loss functions?
In the dataset summary table, how many samples were generated for the 1D advection PDE?
What spatial resolution does the 2D compressible Navier-Stokes dataset use in the summary table?
Which machine learning baseline models does PDEBench provide results for?
How many distinct PDEs and how many datasets does the PDEBench suite cover?
Is the 2D Darcy flow dataset time-dependent in the dataset summary table?
